items.title: Multimodal Continuous Reasoning via Asymmetric Mutual Variational Learning
items.summary: This research introduces a novel framework for multimodal continuous reasoning that leverages asymmetric mutual variational learning to enhance AI's ability to process and understand diverse data types.

items.title: Breaking Failure Cascades: Step-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Medical Multimodal Reasoning
items.summary: A new reinforcement learning approach is proposed to prevent failure cascades in medical multimodal reasoning, improving reliability in critical AI applications.

items.title: ASPIRE: Agentic Skills Discovery for Robotics
items.summary: ASPIRE presents a new method for robots to autonomously discover and acquire skills, potentially accelerating the development of more capable and adaptable robotic systems.

items.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
items.summary: Midjourney is pushing Hollywood studios to disclose their specific applications of AI, signaling growing industry scrutiny and potential legal or ethical debates around AI in creative fields.

items.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
items.summary: Alibaba has reportedly prohibited its employees from using Claude Code, highlighting internal corporate policies and security concerns surrounding third-party AI coding assistants.

items.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
items.summary: Nous Research has released NousCoder-14B, an open-source coding model, offering an alternative to proprietary solutions like Claude Code amidst growing demand for transparent and accessible AI development tools.

items.title: Logit-Contribution Scoring Identifies Non-Literal Retrieval Heads
items.summary: New research introduces a method called Logit-Contribution Scoring to identify and understand the behavior of non-literal retrieval heads in AI models, offering insights into model interpretability.

items.title: SkillCoach: Self-Evolving Rubrics for Evaluating and Enhancing Agentic Skill-Use
items.summary: SkillCoach proposes self-evolving rubrics to improve the evaluation and development of agentic AI skills, crucial for building more competent and autonomous AI agents.

items.title: Optimizing Visual Generative Models via Distribution-wise Rewards
items.summary: This paper explores optimizing visual generative models using distribution-wise rewards, aiming to produce higher quality and more diverse generated content.

items.title: AutoMem: Automated Learning of Memory as a Cognitive Skill
items.summary: AutoMem introduces an automated system for AI to learn and utilize memory as a cognitive skill, potentially leading to more intelligent and context-aware agents.

items.title: GPU Survivors: Can You Survive a 1T Parameter Inference Run?
items.summary: This article discusses the extreme computational challenges and strategies required to perform inference with a trillion-parameter AI model on current GPU hardware.

items.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
items.summary: Google's new commercial showcases AI assisting in the drafting of historical documents like the Declaration of Independence, highlighting the potential for AI in creative and foundational tasks.

items.title: How agents are transforming work
items.summary: OpenAI discusses the evolving role of AI agents in various work environments, detailing their impact on productivity and task automation.

items.title: From SRA to Self-Flow: Data Augmentation or Self-Supervision?
items.summary: This paper investigates the relationship and effectiveness of data augmentation versus self-supervision techniques in AI model training, offering insights into optimal learning strategies.

items.title: Graph-Native Reinforcement Learning Enables Traceable Scientific Hypothesis Generation through Conceptual Recombination
items.summary: Researchers present a graph-native reinforcement learning framework that allows for traceable and interpretable generation of scientific hypotheses by recombining concepts.
